calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SAMER DANFOURA, TEL. NO. 970-8012 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSHUA A. RIDLESS, TEL. NO. 614-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SENAIT AFEWERK and ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND MONEY EXECUTION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS Denied. CCP 473.5 is not applicable to the instant facts, as it pertains only when service is proper but there is excusable neglect. Defendants have failed to show their lack of actual knowledge is not due to inexcusable neglect or avoidance of service. Instead, defendants contend there was no service at all, and that the proofs of service were fraudulently created. See e.g. Brown & Weil, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 5:435 (2010). Moving parties have not sustained their proof on the issue, indeed submitting documents (the Bible school sign out sheet) which appears to have been materially altered to reflect a time consistent with defendants' current position. Furthermore, defendants provide nothing in support of the requirement that they have a "meritorious defense," nor did they proceed with diligence. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/CK)
